text.title: gha-caching-artifacts Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gha-caching-artifacts. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Cache keys MUST include hashFiles('**/lockfile') so the cache is invalidated when dependencies change. Never use a bare runner.os key without a content hash.
rule.rationale: A cache key without a lockfile hash will restore a stale node_modules or site-packages that does not match the current lockfile, causing hard-to-diagnose test failures and version drift.

rule.statement: Set retention-days: 7 on intermediate build artifacts. Reserve the 90-day default only for release artifacts that must be downloadable post-deployment.
rule.rationale: Every run's artifacts consume storage billed to the org. 7-day retention for intermediate outputs keeps storage costs proportional to actual usage.

rule.statement: Use actions/cache@v4 (not v3). The v4 service was rewritten in Feb 2025; v3 silently fails to restore on new runner images. Verify cache hits in the job log after upgrading.
rule.rationale: The v3 → v4 API change broke existing restore logic silently. You lose the entire performance benefit of caching without any error to diagnose.

rule.statement: For Docker Buildx: use cache-from: type=gha and cache-to: type=gha,mode=max. Do not use a manual actions/cache step for Docker layers.
rule.rationale: type=gha is the native GHA cache backend for Buildx; it handles layer deduplication correctly. Manual cache steps for Docker layers corrupt layer ordering.
